top_candidates.reasoning: Three verse-specific markers: (1) the thicket-crossing image — 'crossed the moha-kalilam' appears in the engineering body and the analog operationalizes it as a temporal transition (then-vs-now: three years ago vs today); (2) the temporal scope of nirveda — both already-heard (śrutasya) and yet-to-be-heard (śrotavyasya) is rendered explicitly via 'each new framework arrives... structurally identical to the last fifteen' (already-heard) and 'the next week another post lands' (yet-to-be-heard); (3) the natural-not-cynical diagnostic ('she DOES read, DOES evaluate, sometimes adopts') is verse-specific to Shankara's reading of nirveda as natural maturation.

Adjacent verse on the practitioner's relationship to Vedic teaching. Shares the small-vs-large structure. But 2.46's signature is the small-water-pond / flood image (utility comparison); 2.52's is the thicket-crossing (cognitive transition). The analog's transition framing fits 2.52, not 2.46.

2.42-44 critique veda-vāda-rata (those captured by the Vedas' flowery language). 2.52 names the state in which that capture has ended. The analog could be read as adjacent to 2.42, but the architecture-meme thicket vignette is post-capture, not in-capture.
